column3:
Notes
(yellow border indicates a detag, audio fix, or a good fake)
Sounds as though someone took the outro and layered it over the top of the song. Leaked in 2016; circulated via some old compilations and was relatively unheard of, but it's still worth mentioning.
Edit that leaked in May 2026. Kanye's vocals come from a Donda 2 freestyle called "Tsunami" which uses a similar trumpet-heavy instrumental, hence the quite decent combination.
Snippet with Music Mafia tags thrown over the top. It's just "Stunt Mr. Scott" from 'Buddy Rich'.
Made up by Waterfalls. Another version with Kacy on it was also falsely said to be Travis.

column2:
Kacy Hill - All Alone 
(feat. Travis Scott)
column3: Said to be a 'scrapped interlude' with a spoken outro by Travis. Travis's vocals were taken from a narration for a short film made by Nick Knight known as 'The Seven Deadly Sins of Edward Enniful', and the rest is merely an interlude already released on Kacy's 'Like a Woman'. Kacy confirmed that it's fake via Twitter.

column2:
The Weeknd - All I Know [Demo] 
(feat. Travis Scott)
column3: Rumour tweeted about by RodeoTheAlbum, which led to a news article being written. The song doesn't exist.
Solo version of Surf Club's "Go Off"; it's merely an edit and not a demo.
Snippet that contains the lyrics "I just hit a three-peat, fucked the same one from last week / I don't do no old hoes, all I do is bankroll". Fake version made from unused/re-recorded vocal takes.
Leaked as 'V4'; has the final vocals with a mix of the final instrumental and Demo 1's instrumental.

column2:
Lil Uzi Vert - Aye [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. BNYX, Clif Shayne & Brandon Finessin']
column3: AI edit previously on the tracker; missing some adlibs, and the 808 sounds off.
Leaked as 'V3' by someone who owns the session. Contains real unreleased vocals, but isn't a real version.
Looped snippet made using the "bricks" adlib from "Upper Echelon".
Fake open-verse demo made using multitracks.
Not a traditionally 'fake' song, but a remix doesn't actually exist; Quavo had a different song over the beat titled "Bigger Than You" that was mixed into "Butterfly Effect" during a Chase B set.
Made-up April Fools joke from 2025 based on zero evidence that somehow stuck around.

column2:
A$AP Mob - Call the Cops
(feat. A$AP Rocky, Future & Travis Scott)
column3: Appears on a fake tracklist for 'Cozy Tapes Vol. 2: Too Cozy' posted by HipHop-N-More. A snippet for this song does exist, but Travis definitely isn't on it.
AI remake of the original 2022 snippet. Became popular before the song dropped, and was believed to be real by many.
Appeared on streaming services on October 14, 2016. Consisted of leaks, loosies, and features. Travis and Epic Records both confirmed its illegitimacy and it was quickly removed.
Said to be the first Travis and Metro Boomin collaboration, but is actually "$tay" from 'Cruisin' USA' with OG Chess edited out.
Short snippet of an instrumental that circulated around 2013. Fan-made.

column2:
Kanye West - Eternal Life
(feat. Travis Scott)
column3: Made up by Waterfalls.
Fan-made edit created using Music Mafia's "Forgive Me" snippet. Did the rounds a lot in 2017-18.

column2:
Kanye West - FML [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott & Yung Lean)
column3: Snippet, previously available on the tracker, with Yung Lean vocals and a different beat. The beat can be found in a set of leaked stems; Lean says "fuck my life up" in the song, but it's presumed to be a stem edit.
9-minute rough version with Travis testing flows similar to "Marietta" and "Wine and Hennessy"; it's a stem edit that combines random unused vocal takes.

column2:
Kanye West - I Am A God [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott & The Weeknd)
column3: Demo of "I Am A God" said to feature adlibs from Travis. It was untrue.
Very obviously not Travis or Cudi, but has been uploaded to several profiles on YouTube and SoundCloud.

column2:
Sheck Wes - ILMB [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott & Lil Uzi Vert)
column3: Rumor that spread because Sheck Wes added an image of Lil Uzi Vert in the outro of his "ILMB" snippet he posted to Instagram. Uzi never recorded for the song, Sheck did his first takes in a session with Uzi though. Further confirmation was provided by the producer of the song, AM.
Played at some event that Travis was at on October 17, 2021. It's actually a Dougie F song, titled "Anti".
Much longer tracklist posted by Alek in late 2019. Included here as some people still believe it was real; it occasionally does the rounds on Twitter.
Snippet that surfaced through the OnlyFiles leak. Fake; uses unused vocal takes from "Oh Me Oh My".
Listed in music registries with a release date of October 21, 2022, but nothing came of it. Likely some sort of fake, as there no artists named Hazel that it makes sense for Travis to work with.
Real version with the "I don't mind if you don't wanna come over" line edited into the intro.
Edit made by Bugs. Top result on YouTube and reuploaded a few times so it's worth mentioning.
Not 'fake' in the traditional sense, however, the song doesn't exist. LQ snippets of a song led people to think that Travis and Drake were on it, but it's just a solo Swae Lee song named "System".

column2:
Yeat - Nü Money
(feat. Travis Scott & Drake)
column3: Snippet released on April Fools' Day in 2022 by producer BNYX, featuring Travis's "La Flame" adlib and a fake Metro Boomin tag. Reuploaded to several snippet channels despite being proven fake shortly after. The melody may have been utilised during the intro part of the first beatswitch from "Meltdown".

column2:
DJ Snake - Oh Me Oh My [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott)
(AKA: Oh Me)
column3: Leaked July 30, 2019. Has a fake bridge made from unused vocal takes.
Fake snippet leaked in April 2019; tagged as being for the LGBT community. Made using a well-produced instrumental and real unreleased vocal stems from "I'm Fine".
Found in an old compilation. Sounds identical, but has a slightly muddier mix. Presumed to be fake.
A semi-solo edit of Preme's "Palm Trees". Neither version is a leak as some seem to think, it's just a feature and this supposed 'demo' is merely an edit.
Uses AI to remove the .WAV Radio tags.
Fan-made edit of "Wine and Hennessy".

Was on the tracker at one point as I falsely believed it to be the real demo until it resurfaced in August 2020, as it was labelled as a demo in old compilations from around the time it leaked, and on SoundCloud. This is actually just a remaster made by someone on KTT in 2013.

Fan-made album compilation that appeared on Travis Scott's Apple Music and Spotify pages in late 2019. It consisted of some of Travis' most loved leaks at the time, plus "Promethazine". Mentioned here since it caused various news articles to be published.

Fake version made using a combination of the Demo 1 and the released version. A leaked snippet of this had Music Mafia tags, which would be more than enough proof if it didn't sound so obviously fake.

Not really talked about much, but an old snippet channel has a video of what is possibly an unreleased Travis song titled "Rocket" semi-audible in the background - it's actually a song titled "Leg Over" by Mr Eazi.
Instrumental leaked by KTT user xxman in July 2013, supposedly produced with Hudson Mohawke and Kanye West. xxman later said that it's fake.

Fake "remix" containing Uzi vocals, originally said to have been recorded in 2017, leaked in April 2, 2025. The Uzi vocals come from an untitled song made in March 2019, later leaked partially in May 2025.

column2:
Young Thug - She Flexin
(feat. Travis Scott)
column3: Previewed by Thug in 2015. Travis was incorrectly rumoured as a feature.

column2:
Stars On The Roof
(feat. The Weeknd)
column3: Fanmade AI song that went viral on TikTok in 2023.
Snippet posted in 2020 and resurfaced in 2021; features rough vocals over a different instrumental. The vocals were likely taken from the 2019 Grammy's rehearsal leak.

column2:
BBY GOYARD - Take Off
(feat. Travis Scott)
column3: A version of the 'Astroworld' throwaway with a verse from BBY GOYARD leaked in 2021, but it's unclear how he came into possession of the track. He teased a snippet of his verse plus some unheard Travis parts before it had leaked, so he obviously had it beforehand, but it's extremely unlikely that he got it officially.

Snippet posted in 2024 that previewed an NLE Choppa verse over the "Take Off" instrumental. If these NLE vocals are any real, they were definitely not sent to Travis or affiliated with him in any way.

column2:
Telescope
(feat. Playboi Carti)
[prod. Twxn, LL Clawz & 507Kaz]
column3: Fanmade AI song that went viral on TikTok across 2023 and 2024. Was also used in a now-deleted promotional video by Beach! Please Festival in 2024, which announced Travis as their headlining act.
Snippet from August 2016. Sounds like Travis in the snippet, but it's actually Quavo; the real song is "Bag on Em" by Migo Domingo, released in April 2018.

column2:
The Kid LAROI - Therapy
(feat. 2 Chainz & Travis Scott)
[prod. Miracle]
column3: Appeared in a list files shown by either Miracle or Laroi. It's just a remix of "4AM" with a Laroi verse and different production.

column2: Kid Cudi - Through the Late Night [Demo]
column3: Fake open-verse demo made using multitracks and unused vocal takes.

column2:
Too Many Chances (Remix)
(feat. The Kid LAROI)
column3: Unofficial remix, recorded after Laroi downloaded the leak from YouTube.
Demo that leaked as "act" in April 2019. Stem edit combining several vocal takes.

Edit that loops the snippet to make a longer song and adds fanmade drums over it. Was unofficially fan-uploaded to the Travis Scott Spotify page in 2021. Deserves a mention since it's believed to be real by many people to this day.

A solo demo leaked in 2019 that features new reference vocals from Travis. This was a bounce mistake; the vocals comes from two separate demos that were accidentally combined when being unofficially exported.
Version that leaked on June 26, 2019. The real one has a rough one-take mumble verse, while this one has a partially finished verse. It's a fake version made using unused and re-recorded vocal takes.
Instrumental leaked by KTT user xxman in July 2013, supposedly produced with Hit-Boy. xxman later stated that it's fake.
Snippet surfaced at the end of July 2023. It's an AI fake. Previously available on the tracker for sometime
